description: live_momentum re-entry-after-loss circuit breaker (N=1 on losses)

metrics.name: reentry_blocked rejects in decisions.jsonl
metrics.expectation: >=1 per loss-after-loss attempt; track >=10 in 14d for re-eval (Session 90 watch-list)

metrics.name: after-win re-entry direction
metrics.expectation: n=5 mixed; tighten if >=3/5 are losses

metrics.name: per-sport distribution of reentry_blocked rejects
metrics.expectation: if all cluster in one sport, evaluate per-sport MOMENTUM_REENTRY_LOSS_LIMIT override

description: vig_stack family cap enforced at executor entry boundary

metrics.name: new position_over_family_cap flags after this ship
metrics.expectation: 0; any fresh post-Session-92 flag means executor-level cap enforcement regressed or a non-vig_stack path is mislabeled

metrics.name: cap_exceeded_reject rejects in decisions.jsonl
metrics.expectation: >=1 only when stale/manual/malformed vig_stack sizing exceeds family cap; normal scanner path should size down before this guard

description: vig_stack disabled on KXHIGHCHI and KXINX at executor entry boundary

metrics.name: family_disabled_reject events in decisions.jsonl
metrics.expectation: >=1 per scan that surfaces a KXHIGHCHI or KXINX vig_stack opportunity; 0 in 14d means the guard is dead code or scan stopped surfacing these families

metrics.name: new vig_stack positions opened on KXHIGHCHI or KXINX after ship
metrics.expectation: 0; any post-Session-93 entry on these families means the disable regressed

metrics.name: 30-day rolling P&L counterfactual: would-have-traded KXHIGHCHI/KXINX vig_stack
metrics.expectation: tracked manually via paper_trades.json filter; if positive sustained, reconsider re-enable; if negative or flat, disable validated

description: live_momentum disabled on atp + nba_game at live_watcher entry (breakeven-WR framework)

metrics.name: new live_momentum positions opened on atp or nba_game after ship
metrics.expectation: 0; any post-Session-97 entry on these sports means the disable regressed

metrics.name: sport_disabled rejects on atp + nba_game in decisions.jsonl
metrics.expectation: >=1 per scan that surfaces an atp or nba_game live_momentum opportunity; rising count means the entry guard is firing on live cohorts

metrics.name: shadow_trades.jsonl rows on atp + nba_game blocked_reason=sport_disabled
metrics.expectation: rising across 14d (Session 95 wiring auto-emits on each sport_disabled reject); if >=20 shadow rows hit settlement with aggregate WR > 55%, the disable was wrong and re-enable warranted; if WR < 40%, disable is data-confirmed

metrics.name: ufc live_momentum settled trade count (watch-list trigger)
metrics.expectation: re-run the breakeven analysis when N >= 15; currently n=11 with -23.9pp gap on lifetime but post-fix data sparse

description: snapshot_universe outer asyncio.wait_for timeout in _main_loop (600s hard cap)

metrics.name: SCAN CYCLE interval median over 24h (awake-hours window)
metrics.expectation: approx 30 min during awake hours under healthy Kalshi; pre-ship median was ~73 min over 48h (Phase 0.1 evidence). If post-ship median stays > 50 min during awake hours with snapshot_outer_timeout_count_24h == 0, a secondary cause exists (likely sync Kalshi calls in _main_loop steps 4-7b) and a Session 99 audit is warranted.

metrics.name: snapshot_outer_timeout_count_24h in bot_state.json
metrics.expectation: 0 under healthy Kalshi; >0 confirms the outer guard fired and abandoned a stuck scan. Counter resets daily alongside scans_today. If consistently >= 3/day for >= 3 days, Kalshi has degraded enough that per-request HTTP timeouts in agent/kalshi_client.py become the next ship.

metrics.name: longest SCAN CYCLE gap during awake hours
metrics.expectation: < 90 min after Session 98 (600s outer cap + 60s recovery sleep + 30 min IDLE wait = ~41 min max + slack). Pre-ship: 479 min observed May 10 03:08-11:07 UTC. If post-ship gaps still exceed 90 min, the outer timeout isn't firing as expected — inspect bot.log for the new ERROR line 'snapshot_universe outer-deadline 600s exceeded'.

description: live_momentum scalar fair-value proxy (estimated_win_prob) — Data Collection Backlog Priority 3 instrumentation, no sizing changes

metrics.name: live_momentum decisions with non-null estimated_win_prob in decisions.jsonl.extra
metrics.expectation: rising forward; non-null when wp_edge + leader_price both present (entry-decision sites at live_watcher.py:1422-1453). Stays null at scan-time sites where game_ctx=None — that's correct, not a regression.

metrics.name: live_momentum paper_trades records carrying estimated_win_prob
metrics.expectation: rising forward on accepted live_momentum trades. paper_trades.json filter: type==live_momentum AND estimated_win_prob present. Pre-Session-99 trades won't carry the field (forward-only).

metrics.name: tools/calibration_report.py renders live_momentum proxy section
metrics.expectation: yes; pre-trades shows 'ships forward-only; expect first calibration after ~14d' caveat; post-trades renders Brier + bias + bucket table

metrics.name: vig_stack records spillover of estimated_win_prob (regression-lock)
metrics.expectation: 0; any vig_stack paper_trades.json record carrying estimated_win_prob means a non-live_watcher writer started populating paper_estimated_win_prob in its opp dict. Investigate the writer; vig_stack should never produce this field.

metrics.name: Brier score on resolved live_momentum proxy calibration (watch-list trigger)
metrics.expectation: re-evaluate proxy model after 14d (target 2026-05-25). If Brier <= 0.20 across >= 30 resolved trades, the proxy is calibrated enough to consider confidence-weighted sizing in a future session. Brier > 0.25 means worse-than-random — investigate the v1 math identity (likely a game_context.win_probability quality issue, not a proxy bug).

description: vig_stack ladder context instrumentation (Data Collection Backlog Priority 5) — family, ladder_total_yes_sum_cents, rung_count, selected_rung_rank_asc+desc, rung_strike, rung_kind, no_price_cents, forecast_bucket_distance, time_to_close_hr, source_forecast_temp, source_city, ladder_context_source. No behavior changes; forward-only.

metrics.name: vig_stack paper_trades records carrying family + rung_count + selected_rung_rank_asc
metrics.expectation: rising forward on accepted vig_stack trades. Filter: type=='vig_stack' AND 'family' in row. Pre-Session-100 records won't carry the fields (forward-only).

metrics.name: weather vig_stack records carrying forecast_bucket_distance
metrics.expectation: rising forward when NWS forecast is cached at scan time; omitted otherwise. Filter: type=='vig_stack' AND family startswith 'KXHIGH' AND 'forecast_bucket_distance' in row.

metrics.name: vig_stack accept decisions in decisions.jsonl carrying ladder context in extra
metrics.expectation: non-zero from first post-restart scan; family/rung_count/selected_rung_rank_asc present on accept decisions (decision=='accept'); reject decisions unchanged (gate-specific extras only).

metrics.name: tools/calibration_report.py renders vig_stack ladder shapes section
metrics.expectation: yes; pre-trades shows 'No resolved vig_stack trades carrying ladder context yet' forward-only caveat; post-trades renders Section A (per-family × rank) and Section B (per-weather-family × forecast_distance bucket).

metrics.name: live_momentum records spillover of family/rung_count (regression-lock)
metrics.expectation: 0; any live_momentum paper_trades row carrying 'family' (or any other LADDER_CONTEXT_KEYS field) means a non-vig_stack writer started populating paper_family. Mirrors Session 99's vig_stack spillover guard.

metrics.name: KXHIGHAUS forecast-edge loss concentration (watch-list trigger)
metrics.expectation: re-evaluate after 14d (target 2026-05-25). If post-Session-100 KXHIGHAUS rows show N>=10 in the [-0.5, 0.5) forecast_distance bucket AND win_rate < 60%, surface as a candidate for Session 101 forecast-edge-aware floor/sizing decision. Pure observation; no action this session. Motivating evidence: two -$199 KXHIGHAUS B-bucket losses (B80.5, B89.5) on 2026-05-09/10 where the day's high landed inside the bucket.

description: _kalshi_get total wall-clock timeout via daemon-thread guard (30s cap). Bounds the slow-drip case where urlopen's per-recv timeout=10 is bypassed by trickle responses, restoring inner-deadline effectiveness in snapshot_universe. Brief was re-scoped mid-session: original directive listed snapshot_universe as out-of-scope based on counter-absent inference, but Phase 0 evidence (bot.log lines 53934/54338/54349) showed today's gap WAS snapshot_universe with the outer wait_for failing silently. Layer 1 only; Layer 2 (outer wait_for binding fix) deferred to Session 102 with watch-list trigger.

metrics.name: longest SCAN CYCLE gap in last 24h during awake hours
metrics.expectation: < 60 min after Session 101. Pre-ship: 1h 59min (13:22-15:21 EDT) AND 2h 6min (15:21-17:28 EDT) on 2026-05-11 — two ~2h gaps in 4h. Post-ship expect inner deadline (300s) to bound to ~5min normal, ~5-10min worst-case. If gaps still > 60min within 14d, the Layer 1 daemon-thread guard isn't bounding the leak — investigate per the watch-list.
metrics.current_value: n/a (pre-observation; first post-restart SCAN CYCLE not yet logged)

metrics.name: median SCAN CYCLE interval during awake hours
metrics.expectation: approx 30 min (config IDLE = 1800s) with ≤30s overshoot per Step 9 wall-clock sleep tolerance. Pre-ship median was severely degraded by the multi-hour gaps. Post-ship target: ≤ 35 min median across rolling 24h awake-hours window.

metrics.name: count of '_kalshi_get total wall-clock timeout' log entries in last 24h
metrics.expectation: 0-10 under normal Kalshi health; non-zero confirms the new daemon-thread guard is firing on individual hung calls. Higher counts during degradation events are expected and a feature (the guard is doing its job). If sustained > 50/day, Kalshi is severely degraded — flag in the next session brief.

metrics.name: Layer 2 watch-list — outer wait_for binding fix (Session 102 candidate)
metrics.expectation: If snapshot_outer_timeout_count_24h in bot_state.json remains absent for >=7 days while the new '_kalshi_get total wall-clock timeout' log line fires non-zero times during the same window, the Session 98 outer guard is provably broken (it should fire when inner-guard activity sustained enough to overshoot 600s). Open Session 102 with the asyncio.get_event_loop() -> asyncio.get_running_loop() migration on Python 3.14 (deprecation hypothesis) plus a regression test for the actual production binding scenario.

description: live_momentum cohort + breakeven deep dive — Outcome C (documentation-only ship). Phase 0 evaluated still-enabled live_momentum sports (NHL, IPL, UFC), the S90 re-entry breaker counterfactual, and S96 context cohort slicing. No single highest-confidence intervention surfaced: UFC at n=11 is below S97's stated N>=15 re-eval threshold; S90 counterfactual is uncomputable due to sizing-unavailable on shadow rows (substrate gap); S96 cohort matched N=2 (too thin). S97 enforcement verified working (post-S97-ship ATP attempts all reject with sport_disabled). No code changes; comprehensive documentation in CLAUDE-sessions.md Session 102 block.

metrics.name: UFC settled live_momentum trade count (S97 watch-list carryover)
metrics.expectation: re-run breakeven analysis when N >= 15; currently n=11 with -23.9pp lifetime gap vs BE. If at N=15 the lifetime WR is still below BE by >=5pp AND the post-S97-ship sub-cohort isn't clearly profitable, ship a follow-on UFC disable session.

metrics.name: post-Apr-23 settled live_momentum cohort size (S41 watch-list trigger)
metrics.expectation: S41's N>=60 threshold met at N=66 on 2026-05-11; queue a Session 103+ candidate to re-run S41's TP/SL training sweep on the expanded cohort and check whether the SL-axis-flat finding still holds. If a new TP winner emerges that's an actionable Outcome A.

metrics.name: settled live_momentum trades with S96 context matched in decisions.jsonl
metrics.expectation: expected >=20 by 2026-05-14 (~3 days post-S96 ship); re-run S102 0.3 cohort-slicing on leader_price / dip_cents / spread_cents / volume_24h / completion buckets at that point. Today's matched count was N=2 (architectural gap: S96 emits context only on per-tick path with game_ctx available, not all 6 scan-time sites).

metrics.name: shadow_trades.jsonl reentry_blocked rows with sizing_status=available (S90 substrate gap)
metrics.expectation: 0 today; remains 0 until a future session wires pre-block sizing into bot/live_watcher.py's reentry-reject path. Without computable counterfactual P&L (would_contracts / would_notional are null on all reentry_blocked shadow rows today), S90's N=1 threshold cannot be data-validated. Watch-list trigger: if S90 fires >=10 times across 14d without sizing wiring, prioritize the substrate fix.

metrics.name: S97 enforcement on atp + nba_game (Session 102 0.4 verification carry-forward)
metrics.expectation: 0 new live_momentum accepts on atp or nba_game post-S97-ship. Verified working on 2026-05-11: 4 ATP attempts on 2026-05-11 all rejected with reason=sport_disabled. Regression check if any new accepts appear.
